page: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the processor demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests and Geekbench single-core scores. Multi-core performance is also strong, with Geekbench multi-core scores in the top 10%, though stress-ng multi-core performance falls into the middle 50%. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX-VNNI, and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. Single-threaded compression and decompression benchmarks, such as gzip and bzip3, register in the top 10% tier, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput rank in the top 25%.

Memory and database benchmarks, including Passmark memory scores and Redis SET operations, perform in the average tier. For machine learning workloads, the instance achieves top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds with small LLM models (135M parameters), but drops to average performance when processing medium (7B parameter) models. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which represents a key architectural tradeoff for I/O-heavy workloads.

The c8a.4xlarge is structured for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores and high clock speeds without requiring massive memory footprints or local disk space. Appropriate workloads include single-threaded or moderately parallelized compute tasks, web serving, software compilation, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying on an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the server excels in single-core execution, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, as well as single-threaded compression. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall into the average tier. This hardware profile is optimized for compute-heavy applications, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference where high clock speeds and dedicated CPU resources are required, presenting a balanced cost-to-compute ratio for non-storage-intensive workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, designed for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge features 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for compute workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86 server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores, high-speed DDR5 memory, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz
32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for compute-intensive tasks, web serving, and small LLM inference
